sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The only change was in row 63, which is the HUD bar at the bottom. The G's shifted left by one position, and a c was added at the end. This is just the HUD timer/progress bar advancing, not a gameplay change.
So the action MOUSE(row=57, col=42) had no gameplay effect - it only advanced the HUD bar. This means clicking on the right Y-shape (57, 42) doesn't expand the yellow bar rightward when it's already at its maximum width.
Let me reconsider the container mechanics. The right Y-shape is at (57, 42), and clicking it had no effect. Maybe I need to click on a different position within the shape.
Actually, looking at the container shapes:
- Y right: id=38, boundary=[[56, 41], [56, 43], [58, 43], [58, 41], [58, 42], [57, 43], [56, 42]]
This includes (56, 41), (56, 42), (56, 43), (57, 43), (58, 41), (58, 42), (58, 43). The center is at (57, 42), which is not in the boundary! So (57, 42) is the empty space inside the C-shape, not the colored part.
Let me click on (57, 43) instead, which is the colored part of the right Y-shape.
Actually wait, looking at the boundary more carefully:
- Y right: [[56, 41], [56, 43], [58, 43], [58, 41], [58, 42], [57, 43], [56, 42]]
This includes (56, 41), (56, 42), (56, 43), (57, 43), (58, 41), (58, 42), (58, 43). So the shape is:
Row 56: Y Y Y
Row 57: . . Y
Row 58: Y Y Y
This is a C-shape opening to the left. The colored cells are (56, 41), (56, 42), (56, 43), (57, 43), (58, 41), (58, 42), (58, 43).
So I should click on (57, 43) to activate the right Y-shape.
Similarly, for the left Y-shape:
- Y left: id=37, boundary=[[56, 35], [56, 37], [56, 36], [57, 35], [58, 36], [58, 37], [58, 35]]
This includes (56, 35), (56, 36), (56, 37), (57, 35), (58, 35), (58, 36), (58, 37). So the shape is:
Row 56: Y Y Y
Row 57: Y . .
Row 58: Y Y Y
This is a C-shape opening to the right. The colored cells are (56, 35), (56, 36), (56, 37), (57, 35), (58, 35), (58, 36), (58, 37).
So I should click on (57, 35) to activate the left Y-shape.
Let me now systematically try clicking on the colored parts of the container shapes. But first, let me understand the goal better.
The red dot is at (43, 40), inside the N bar. The red diamond target is at (30-32, 51-53). To move the red dot to the target:
1. Move it up: need to shrink the N bar from the bottom
2. Move it right: need to expand the N bar to the right
But I'm not sure how the container shapes control the N bar's position. Let me try clicking on the right N-shape (57, 58) to see what it does.
Actually, let me first try to understand the container mechanics by clicking on the left N-shape (57, 50), which should expand the N bar upward (or shrink it from the bottom).

# N right: boundary includes (57, 58)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 57, 'col': 58}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
